title: Gear for a 50 lb Dog Who Runs Hard and Fetches Everything
intro: Your dog is athletic and ball-obsessed — the gear needs to keep up with daily fetch sessions and trail runs without falling apart in a month.

items.title: West Paw Jive Dog Ball, Large
items.note: The ball the fetch community reaches for when tennis balls shred in a week — Zogoflex holds up to hard chewers, bounces unpredictably, and floats, made in the USA.

items.title: Chuckit! Ultra Ball
items.note: The default recommendation in high-drive dog communities for a reason — high-rubber-core construction survives launcher use and compulsive retrievers far longer than standard balls.

items.title: Ruffwear Web Master Harness
items.note: The harness avalanche rescue teams and service dog handlers have trusted for two decades — four adjustment points, a perforated foam panel, and a reinforced handle make it the proven choice for working dogs and serious trail runners.

items.title: Ruffwear Trail Runner Ultralight Dog Bowl
items.note: At 18 grams it stuffs into a jacket pocket and holds a full liter — what the fast-and-light crowd reaches for when they don't want dead weight between water breaks.

items.title: Ruffwear Bivy Collapsible Dog Bowl
items.note: For longer outings where a 50 lb dog needs serious volume — welded single-wall construction holds 1.8L and packs flat, no soggy fabric or collapsed sides mid-drink.

warnings: WUNDERball sourced from gowacky.us — unfamiliar retailer with minimal independent reputation signal; dropped pending community validation.
Chuckit! Ultra Ball price is in CAD from a Canadian retailer — US pricing should be verified before publishing.
The REI category-page URLs for harnesses and bowls were dropped — they resolve to browse pages, not specific product listings.
Wilderdog collapsible bottle is a human water bottle adapted for dog use, not a dog bowl — context unclear for this audience; dropped.
No dedicated running leash or hands-free belt in the extracted data — a significant gap for a dog described as a trail runner.
